markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Allow operator annotations/editable overlays without making generated projection text canonical authority. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: WikiPageEditableOverlay leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: WikiPageEditableOverlay is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Operator annotation overlays on wiki projections WITHOUT making the generated projection canonical. The overlay is its OWN authority row; the projection stays non-authority and regenerable.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0295_loom_wiki_overlays.sql (NEW) - loom_wiki_overlays (annotation = authority; FK->knowledge_wiki_projections ON DELETE CASCADE; no authority FK FROM any record)

evidence.result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed (real PostgreSQL) [shared MT-184/185/187 suite]
tests: overlay_is_own_authority_independent_of_projection - adding an overlay does NOT change the projection rendered_content; overlay survives projection regenerate; deleting the overlay does not touch the projection

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 043cf8c8.

findings: OVERLAY-NOT-AUTHORITY-LEAK (handled+tested): the projection rendered_content is never modified by an overlay; the overlay is a separate loom_wiki_overlays authority row. Proven: projection content unchanged after add; overlay survives regenerate.

INDEPENDENT LIFECYCLE (handled+tested): deleting an overlay leaves the projection intact; the overlay FK ON DELETE CASCADE means a projection delete also removes its overlays without touching any authority record.
EMPTY ANNOTATION (handled): add_loom_wiki_overlay rejects a blank annotation (Validation); DB also has a btrim<>'' CHECK.
WORKSPACE SCOPING + FAIL-CLOSED (handled): the projection must exist in the workspace before an overlay can be added; delete returns NotFound for a missing overlay.
fixes: No code change required; findings handled-by-design and tested.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-185 (WikiPageEditableOverlay) requires operator annotations on generated wiki projections WITHOUT the regenerable projection becoming canonical authority — the overlay must be its own authority row, backed by PostgreSQL/EventLedger. The code delivers this: migration 0295 creates loom_wiki_overlays (annotation = authority, FK->projection ON DELETE CASCADE, no authority FK FROM any record), Postgres impls add/list/delete with workspace scoping + fail-closed projection existence, HTTP routes are wired, and a real-PostgreSQL test proves the projection text is never mutated by an overlay, the overlay survives projection regenerate, and deleting the overlay leaves the projection intact. Aligns with the current Master Spec projection-never-authority law.
findings: Migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0295_loom_wiki_overlays.sql:14-37 creates loom_wiki_overlays with annotation TEXT NOT NULL (authority), projection_id FK->knowledge_wiki_projections ON DELETE CASCADE, workspace_id FK->workspaces, btrim(annotation)<>'' CHECK, and the comment correctly documents the projection-never-authority split. Down migration is clean.
storage/loom.rs:1275-1289 defines LoomWikiOverlay (authority struct) with anchor doc'd as 'never an absolute path' per MT-152 typed-reference discipline.

Real Postgres impls at storage/postgres.rs:10542-10615: add validates non-empty annotation, fail-closes on missing projection via get_loom_wiki_projection, uses Uuid::now_v7() ids; list is workspace+projection scoped; delete returns NotFound when 0 rows affected. These are genuine SQL, not stubs.

The NotImplemented returns at storage/mod.rs:2779-2805 are DEFAULT methods on `pub trait Database` (mod.rs:1964), overridden by PostgresDatabase. AppState.storage is Postgres-backed (api/loom.rs:1181), so NotImplemented is NOT reachable from the live path — standard trait-default-then-override pattern, not a deferred-live escape.
API routes wired at api/loom.rs:181-186 (GET/POST .../loom/wiki/:id/overlays, DELETE .../loom/wiki-overlays/:id) with ensure_workspace_exists guard (api/loom.rs:1180).

Runtime proof: tests/loom_wiki_boundary_tests.rs:181-221 overlay_is_own_authority_independent_of_projection asserts projection.rendered_content unchanged after overlay add, overlay not folded into projection, overlay survives regenerate, projection survives overlay delete. Harness (knowledge_pg_support.rs) runs against REAL PostgreSQL only (managed cluster / DATABASE_URL), fresh isolated schema with full migration chain, explicit no-SQLite/no-mock/no-in-memory fallback (returns None+SKIP only when PG binaries truly absent). Commit 043cf8c8 records 5 passed on real PostgreSQL.
No reachable todo!/unimplemented!/mock/fake/SQLite hits on the impl or test live path.

Aside (non-blocking): KERNEL_BUILDER both implemented and self-reviewed (lifecycle.adversarial_code_review reviewer = self), but the runtime proof is real PostgreSQL exercising the actual contract, so sub-rule 3 self-cert is not a fail. Packet cites v02.192 spec anchors (current is v02.194) — expected winding-down paperwork drift, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
